title: eurybia paludosa
intro: Eurybia paludosa, also known as the marsh lowry's eurybia or marsh eurybia, is a type of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae, native to North America. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to reduce inflammation and ease digestive issues due to its content of sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and other bioactive compounds. Eurybia paludosa has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory problems, as well as to promote wound healing and improve overall well-being. Herbal preparations of the plant, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, can be made from its roots, leaves, and flowers, and are often used in folk medicine and natural remedies.
